events.output: Found 8 existing comprehension artifacts. workflow-server-schemas.md and workflow-server.md directly relevant.

Reviewed workflow-server-schemas.md (380 lines): covers dual-schema design, field propagation lifecycle, additionalProperties policy, Mode pattern as template, extension surface. Reviewed workflow-server.md (262 lines): covers architecture, tool call lifecycle, design patterns.
All 10 target files analyzed. Architecture well-understood from prior activities. Key insight: get_skills handler at resource-tools.ts:84-139 is the primary extension point.
readSkill() is the sole server-used export from skill-loader.ts. get_skills extracts skillIds from activity.skills. Workflow schema has executionModel but no skills field.

Skills were activity-scoped because the original design tied skills to activities. Universal skills in meta/ were a later addition for cross-cutting concerns but were never integrated into the get_skills API path.

Workflow-level skills = execution protocol skills (how to run). Activity-level skills = domain skills (what to do). This distinction was implicit; making it explicit via schema field makes the architecture self-documenting.

Augmented workflow-server-schemas.md with Deep-Dive 2 (§9): 4 new sections covering get_skills anatomy, schema extension, dead code map, and meta skill update points. Added 3 resolved questions to Open Questions table.

All 3 new open questions resolved through code analysis: Q6 (get_skills branching), Q7 (dead code inventory), Q8 (findWorkflowsWithSkills is NOT dead). skill-loader.test.ts analyzed: 7 tests to remove, 11 to keep.
All open questions resolved. has_open_questions=false, needs_comprehension=false. Comprehension sufficient — skipping sufficiency checkpoint per SUFFICIENCY RULE.

user_corrections: Workflow-level skills should NOT be semantically constrained as 'how-to-run'. Same flexibility as activity-level skills.
executionModel may become unnecessary after workflow-level skills are added.

events.output: Applied 2 corrections: (1) removed 'how to run vs what to do' semantic constraint from §9.2, (2) added §9.5 executionModel deprecation analysis and §9.6 corrections log to comprehension artifact.
